---
title: "Roads Project 07-28-2025"
author: "Will Jones"
editor: visual
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  publish:
  resources:
  - "*.png"
  site-url: "https://willj98.quarto.pub/roads-project-07-28"
---

# Data Visualization for Our Roads Distribution Study

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
#| message: false
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(nngeo)
library(leaflet)
library(leaflegend)
library(tigris)
library(foreign)
library(purrr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggridges)
library(tmap)
library(landscapemetrics)
```

### Read in and tidy our pairwise point data

This table includes the PAIRED distributions of points measured in 2000 and 2024. These distances are measured between each \~1e6 point and the nearest U.S. Census Bureau TIGER/Line road feature.

`all_data <- st_read('pairwise_data_07_28.gpkg')`

`EPSG:5070, NAD 83 CONUS Albers | Unit: m`

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| include: false

# This table is easy to use for calculating summary statistics of each distribution
setwd("E:\\01_PROJECTS\\01_Roads\\06_fixing_final_files")
all_data <- st_read('pairwise_data_07_28.gpkg')
all_data <- all_data %>% mutate(ratio = (dist_24 / dist_00)) %>% mutate(log_ratio = log10(ratio))
#head(all_data)
# While we pivot this data to "long" format for easier visualization
long_data <- all_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(dist_00, dist_24, log_ratio),
    names_to = "measure_type", 
    values_to = "meters" 
  )
```

Columns common to both measurements ('00 and '24) are `lon, lat, nlcd, lulc, county_name, ws_name, ws_code, ecoregion,`and `eco_code.` The columns `rdcls_00` and`rdcls_24` contain the classification of the nearest road in 2000 and 2024, respectively. Columns `dist_00` and `dist_24` contain the distance in meters to the nearest road in each year. The columns `ratio` and `ln_ratio` are `(dist_24/dist_00)`and `ln(dist_24/dist_00)`, respectively.

```{r}
print(colnames(all_data))
```

<br>

Summary stats for yr 2000 and yr 2024

```{r}
#| echo: false
print('pairwise distribution yr 2000:')
long_data %>% filter(measure_type == 'dist_00') %>% select(meters) %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% summary()
print('pairwise distribution yr 2024:')
long_data %>% filter(measure_type == 'dist_24') %>% select(meters) %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% summary()

```

```{r}

```

```{r fig.height =8, fig.width=10}
#| fig-cap: 'Distribution of Paired Sample Points'

# Sample jitter data separately
jitter_sample <- long_data %>%
  filter(measure_type %in% c('dist_00', 'dist_24')) %>%
  mutate(measure_type = factor(measure_type, levels = c('dist_00', 'dist_24'))) %>%
  filter(
    meters >= quantile(meters, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    meters <= quantile(meters, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  sample_n(1000)  # Smaller sample just for jitter
levels(jitter_sample$measure_type) <- c("2000", "2024")

# Full data for boxplot
box_data <- long_data %>%
  filter(measure_type %in% c('dist_00', 'dist_24')) %>%
  mutate(measure_type = factor(measure_type, levels = c('dist_00', 'dist_24'))) %>%
  filter(
    meters >= quantile(meters, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    meters <= quantile(meters, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
levels(box_data$measure_type) <- c("2000", "2024")

# Create plot
ggplot(box_data, aes(x = measure_type, y = meters)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Suppress default outliers if you like
  geom_jitter(data = jitter_sample, aes(x = measure_type, y = meters), 
              width = 0.15, alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  xlab('Year') +
  ylab('Distance (meters)')


```

```{r}

dbell_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(county_name, measure_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_value = mean(meters), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = measure_type, values_from = avg_value)
```

```{r}

dumbbell <- dbell_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = county_name)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = dist_00, xend = dist_24, yend = county_name), color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_00, y = county_name),
            col = 'purple', alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_24, y = county_name),
            col = 'orange', alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_reverse() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())


ggplotly(dumbbell)
```

```{r}

dbell_data <- long_data %>%
  group_by(ecoregion, measure_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_value = mean(meters), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = measure_type, values_from = avg_value)
```

```{r}

dumbbell <- dbell_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = ecoregion)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = dist_00, xend = dist_24, yend = ecoregion), color = "black", alpha = 0.7,
              ) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_00, y = ecoregion),
            col = 'purple', alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_24, y = ecoregion),
            col = 'orange', alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal()



ggplotly(dumbbell)
```

```{r}
dumbbell <- dbell_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = ecoregion)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = dist_00, xend = dist_24, yend = ecoregion), color = "black", alpha = 0.7,
              arrow = arrow(type='closed')) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_00, y = ecoregion),
           col = 'purple', alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_24, y = ecoregion),
           col = 'orange', alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())

dumbbell
```

```{r fig.height =8, fig.width=10}
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of Paired Points"
pairwise_histogram <- long_data %>%
  filter(measure_type %in% c('dist_00','dist_24'
  )) %>%
  mutate(measure_type = factor(measure_type,
                          levels = c("dist_00", "dist_24"),
                          labels = c("2000", "2024"))) %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = log(meters), fill = measure_type)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.5, bins = 500) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("2000" = "#009688", "2024" = "#FF7043")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  xlab('ln(meters)')

ggplotly(pairwise_histogram)

```

```{r}

log_diff_hist <- long_data %>% filter(measure_type == 'log_ratio') %>% ggplot(aes(x=meters)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 1000)

ggplotly(log_diff_hist)
```

```{r}
eco_ridges <- long_data %>% filter(measure_type == 'dist_24') %>%
ggplot(mapping = aes(x= log(meters), y = ecoregion, fill = ecoregion)) +
geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.6) +
theme_ridges() +
theme(legend.position = "none") +
xlim(0,10)


eco_ridges
  
```

```{r}
# Combine and prepare
long_data_viz <- long_data %>%
  filter(measure_type %in% c("dist_00", "dist_24")) %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      measure_type == "dist_00" ~ "2000",
      measure_type == "dist_24" ~ "2024"
    ),
    ecoregion = fct_reorder(ecoregion, desc(meters), .fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Plot
ggplot(long_data_viz, aes(x = log10(meters), y = ecoregion, fill = period)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.6,
    scale = 0.97,
    stat = "binline",
    bins = 100,
    position = "identity",
    color = NA
  ) +
  theme_ridges() +
  ylab("Ecoregion") +
  xlab("log10(Distance in meters)") +
  labs(fill = "Year") + 
scale_fill_manual(values = c('2000' ='maroon', '2024' ='orange'
))
  ggtitle("Distribution of Distances By Ecoregion (2000 vs 2024)")
```

```{r fig.height =8, fig.width=10}
lulc_00 <- long_data %>% 
  mutate(
    lulc = fct_reorder(lulc, desc(meters), .fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
filter(measure_type %in% c('dist_00')) %>%
ggplot(mapping = aes(x= log10(meters), y = lulc, fill = lulc)) +
geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.6, scale = 0.97, stat = "binline", bins = 100) +
theme_ridges() +
theme(legend.position = "none") +
ylab('Land Use/Land Cover') +
ggtitle('2000')


lulc_00


lulc_24 <- long_data %>% 
  mutate(
    lulc = fct_reorder(lulc, desc(meters), .fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
filter(measure_type %in% c('dist_24')) %>%
ggplot(mapping = aes(x= log1p(meters), y = lulc, fill = lulc)) +
geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.6, scale = 0.97, stat = "binline", bins = 100) +
theme_ridges() +
theme(legend.position = "none") +
ylab('Land Use/Land Cover') +
ggtitle('2024')


lulc_24


```

```{r fig.height =8, fig.width=10}
#| lightbox: true
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)

# Combine and prepare
long_data_viz <- long_data %>%
  filter(measure_type %in% c("dist_00", "dist_24")) %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      measure_type == "dist_00" ~ "2000",
      measure_type == "dist_24" ~ "2024"
    ),
    lulc = fct_reorder(lulc, desc(meters), .fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Plot
ggplot(long_data_viz, aes(x = log1p(meters), y = lulc, fill = period)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha = 0.6,
    scale = 0.97,
    stat = "binline",
    bins = 100,
    position = "identity",
    color = NA
  ) +
  theme_ridges() +
  ylab("Land Use/Land Cover") +
  xlab("log1p(Distance in meters)") +
  labs(fill = "Year") + 
scale_fill_manual(values = c('2000' ='maroon', '2024' ='orange'
))
  ggtitle("Distribution of Distances (2000 vs 2024)")

```

```{r}
#| fig-cap: 'Comparison of Pairwise Points and Their Distance to the Nearest Road in 2000 and 2024'
paired_scatter <- sample_n(all_data, 5000) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(x=dist_00, y =dist_24), alpha = 0.5) +
scale_fill_continuous(type= "viridis") +
xlab("Distance to the nearest road in 2000")+
ylab("Distance to the nearest road in 2024") +
xlim(0,5000) +
ylim(0,5000)

ggplotly(paired_scatter)
```

```{r}

 old_data <- read.csv("F:\\01_PROJECTS\\all_data.csv")

```

```{r}
#| fig.cap = 'Values of Distance to the Nearest Road in 2000 vs 2024, using base data (orange), and snapped data (purple)'
two_scatters <- sample_n(old_data, 5000) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_2k_no_snap, y = dist_24, color = "No Snap"), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_point(aes(x = dist_2k, y = dist_24, color = "Snap"), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("No Snap" = "orange", "Snap" = "purple")) +
  xlab('Distance to the nearest road in 2000') +
  ylab('Distance to the nearest road in 2024') +
  labs(color = "Network Type") +
  xlim(0,5000)+
ylim(0,5000) +
theme_bw()

ggplotly(two_scatters)

```

## Below Figures:

### 1 & 2: Vector (point) values of distance to the nearest road in 2000 (top) and 2024 (bottom). Light: closer, dark: farther

![](yr_00_dot_map.png)

![](yr_24_dot_map.png)

### 3 & 4: Point data aggregated to 1km cells, showing distance to the nearest road in 2000 (top) and 2024 (bottom). Bright (yellow): closer, dark (blue): farther.

![](yr_00_raster_map.png)

![](yr_24_raster_map.png)
